factors.summary: Input costs are decreasing, contributing to margin improvements.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2015, delivery costs decreased 4.6 percent and compensation costs decreased 2.8 percent as compared to the same period in 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are decreasing, leading to margin improvements.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with decreases in compensation costs.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2015, compensation costs decreased 2.8 percent as compared to the same period in 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs have decreased, contributing to cost management.

factors.summary: Management is confident with its strategic direction and financial flexibility.

evidence.text: These actions have improved our financial flexibility and liquidity and positioned us to invest the necessary capital in our business. As of September 30, 2015, we had available liquidity of $ 814, including cash and cash equivalents of $ 171.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is confident with strategic direction and financial flexibility.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include softness in upstream oil and gas sector.

evidence.text: We expect the combination of recent industry fleet expansion and continuing softness in the upstream oil and gas sector to continue to place pressure on volume and pricing, though we expect 2016 to reflect solid industry growth.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include softness in the upstream oil and gas sector.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear and well-resourced.

evidence.text: The expansion of our trench, power and pump business, as well as our tools offering, and the cross-selling of these services throughout our network will further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Long-term strategic direction is clear and well-resourced.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include upstream oil and gas sector performance and currency impacts.

evidence.text: The decreased equipment rental gross margin in our trench, power and pump segment primarily reflects decreased margins in the Pump Solutions region, which is primarily comprised of locations acquired in the National Pump acquisition and which experienced volume and pricing pressure associated with upstream oil and gas customers. The aggregate equipment rentals gross margin in the trench, power and pump segment excluding the Pump Solutions region increased by approximately 1.6 percentage points from 2014. We expect the combination of recent industry fleet expansion and continuing softness in the upstream oil and gas sector to continue to place pressure on volume and pricing, though we expect 2016 to reflect solid industry growth.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities include upstream oil and gas sector performance and currency impacts.

factors.summary: Catalysts include expansion of trench, power, and pump services and Lean management program.

evidence.text: The expansion of our trench, power and pump business, as well as our tools offering, and the cross-selling of these services throughout our network will further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings. We have trained over 3,000 employees, approximately 75 percent of our district managers and over 60 percent of our branch managers on the Lean kaizen process. We continue to implement this program across our branch network, with the objectives of: reducing the cycle time associated with renting our equipment to customers; improving invoice accuracy and service quality; reducing the elapsed time for equipment pickup and delivery; and improving the effectiveness and efficiency of our repair and maintenance operations;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Catalysts include expansion of services and Lean management program implementation.

factors.summary: Market share is stable and well-positioned due to size and resources.

evidence.text: We are the largest equipment rental company in the world, with an integrated network of 900 rental locations in the United States and Canada. Although the equipment rental industry is highly fragmented and diverse, we believe that we are well positioned to take advantage of this environment because, as a larger company, we have more extensive resources and certain competitive advantages.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Market share is stable and well-positioned due to size and resources.

factors.summary: Differentiation is maintained through scale, resources, and service offerings.

evidence.text: We are the largest equipment rental company in the world, with an integrated network of 900 rental locations in the United States and Canada. Although the equipment rental industry is highly fragmented and diverse, we believe that we are well positioned to take advantage of this environment because, as a larger company, we have more extensive resources and certain competitive advantages.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's scale, resources, and service offerings provide a strong competitive advantage, indicating a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to grow with strategic investments.

evidence.text: These actions have improved our financial flexibility and liquidity and positioned us to invest the necessary capital in our business. As of September 30, 2015, we had available liquidity of $ 814, including cash and cash equivalents of $ 171.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strategic investments are supported by improved financial flexibility, signaling a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving with financial restructuring.

evidence.text: During 2015, we took the following actions that have improved our financial flexibility and liquidity: redeemed all of our 5 3/4 percent Senior Secured Notes and 8 3/8 percent Senior Subordinated Notes; redeemed $ 350 principal amount of our 8 1/4 percent Senior Notes; issued $1 billion principal amount of 4 5/8 percent Senior Secured Notes; issued $800 principal amount of 5 1/2 percent Senior Notes; amended and extended our ABL facility, and increased the size of the facility to $2.5 billion; and amended and extended our accounts receivable securitization facility, and increased the size of the facility to $ 625.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Financial restructuring has significantly improved debt leverage, reflecting a very positive development.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities $ 1,557 $ 1,466 Adjustments for items included in net cash provided by operating activities but excluded from the calculation of EBITDA: Amortization of deferred financing costs and original issue discounts (8) (14) Gain on sales of rental equipment 164 161 Gain on sales of non-rental equipment 6 7 Merger related costs (1) 26 (13) Restructuring charge (2) (1) 2 Stock compensation expense, net (3) (37) (48) Loss on repurchase/redemption of debt securities and amendment of ABL facility (123) (80) Excess tax benefits from share-based payment arrangements 57 — Changes in assets and liabilities 57 1 Cash paid for interest 304 315 Cash paid for income taxes, net 55 60 EBITDA $ 2,057 $ 1,857 Add back: Merger related costs (1) (26) 13 Restructuring charge (2) 1 (2) Stock compensation expense, net (3) 37 48 Impact of the fair value mark-up of acquired RSC fleet (4) 19 27 Adjusted EBITDA $ 2,088 $ 1,943

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong cash flow generation and improved EBITDA indicate very positive financial health.

factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, with evidence from both the Results of Operations and Executive Overview subsections. The demand increase is particularly notable in the trench, power, and pump segment, as reflected in the growth in equipment rental volume and revenue.

evidence.text: For the three months ended September 30, 2015, equipment rentals of $1.326 billion increased $11, or 0.8 percent, as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily reflecting a 2.4 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent partially offset by a 0.1 percent rental rate decrease and the adverse impact of currency.
Equipment rental revenue increased 4.9 percent as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily reflecting a 4.2 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent and a 1.3 percent rental rate increase.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Accelerating demand and revenue growth in key segments suggest a positive trend.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed results in terms of pricing power, with overall margin improvements driven by cost efficiencies and successful price increases, though certain segments, particularly the Pump Solutions region, are facing challenges due to volume and pricing pressures.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2015, the aggregate general rentals' equipment rentals gross margin increased 1.5 percentage points to 42.6 percent as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily reflecting cost improvements, partially offset by a 0.5 percentage point decrease in time utilization.

The decreased equipment rental gross margin in our trench, power and pump segment primarily reflects decreased margins in the Pump Solutions region, which is primarily comprised of locations acquired in the National Pump acquisition and which experienced volume and pricing pressure associated with upstream oil and gas customers.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed results with some margin improvements but challenges in certain regions indicate a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the trench, power, and pump business, as well as through the expansion of tools offerings and cross-selling services. This strategic focus is expected to enhance profitability and position the company as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2015, the South region's equipment rentals gross margin increased 1.1 percentage points to 48.4 percent as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily reflecting a 2.0 percent rental rate increase partially offset by a 0.1 percentage point decrease in time utilization.

The expansion of our trench, power and pump business, as well as our tools offering, and the cross-selling of these services throughout our network will further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments and strategic expansion enhance profitability, signaling a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is positive and accelerating, driven by increases in equipment rentals volume and rental rates.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2015, equipment rentals of $3.671 billion increased $172, or 4.9 percent, as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily reflecting a 4.2 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent and a 1.3 percent rental rate increase, partially offset by the adverse impact of currency.
Equipment rental revenue increased 4.9 percent as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily reflecting a 4.2 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent and a 1.3 percent rental rate increase.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive and accelerating organic growth driven by volume and rate increases reflects a very positive development.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by real demand, with evidence of a 2.4 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent in the Results of Operations subsection. However, the Executive Overview subsection highlights a slightly higher 4.2 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent, contributing to overall growth despite a rental rate decrease in one subsection.

evidence.text: For the three months ended September 30, 2015, equipment rentals of $1.326 billion increased $11, or 0.8 percent, as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily reflecting a 2.4 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent partially offset by a 0.1 percent rental rate decrease and the adverse impact of currency.
Equipment rental revenue increased 4.9 percent as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily reflecting a 4.2 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent and a 1.3 percent rental rate increase.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, driven by real demand, with evidence of a 2.4 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent.

factors.summary: The end markets in regions like the South are experiencing strong growth due to a faster demand recovery, with the South region's equipment rentals gross margin being 13 percent higher than aggregated general rentals' regions. Additionally, the construction and industrial customer segments are growing the fastest, reflecting positive momentum in these areas.

evidence.text: For the five year period ended September 30, 2015, the South region's equipment rentals gross margin of 46.0 percent was 13 percent more than the equipment rentals gross margins of the aggregated general rentals' regions over the same period. The South region's equipment rentals gross margin was more than the other general rentals' regions during this period due to strong end markets that have recovered faster than in other parts of the country.
Our strategy focuses on serving large construction and industrial customers, as well as select local contractors, and we believe these efforts will lead to even better service of our target accounts.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: End markets in regions like the South are experiencing strong growth due to a faster demand recovery, with the South region's equipment rentals gross margin being 13 percent higher than aggregated general rentals' regions.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance, with overall gross margins expanding due to cost improvements and rental rate increases, while certain segments, particularly the trench, power, and pump segment, are experiencing margin compression due to pricing and volume pressures.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2015, the aggregate general rentals' equipment rentals gross margin increased 1.5 percentage points to 42.6 percent as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily reflecting cost improvements, partially offset by a 0.5 percentage point decrease in time utilization.

The decreased equipment rental gross margin in our trench, power and pump segment primarily reflects decreased margins in the Pump Solutions region, which is primarily comprised of locations acquired in the National Pump acquisition and which experienced volume and pricing pressure associated with upstream oil and gas customers.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance, with overall gross margins expanding due to cost improvements and rental rate increases, while certain segments are experiencing margin compression.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost reduction programs that are delivering measurable savings. These programs include training employees on Lean kaizen processes to improve operational efficiency and reduce costs.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2015, delivery costs decreased 4.6 percent and compensation costs decreased 2.8 percent as compared to the same period in 2014.

We have trained over 3,000 employees, approximately 75 percent of our district managers and over 60 percent of our branch managers on the Lean kaizen process. We continue to implement this program across our branch network, with the objectives of: reducing the cycle time associated with renting our equipment to customers; improving invoice accuracy and service quality; reducing the elapsed time for equipment pickup and delivery; and improving the effectiveness and efficiency of our repair and maintenance operations;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has implemented cost reduction programs that are delivering measurable savings, including training employees on Lean kaizen processes to improve operational efficiency and reduce costs.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through both cost reduction and efficiency initiatives, as well as Lean management techniques. The evidence highlights specific operational improvements, including increased gross margins and enhanced service quality.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2015, the aggregate general rentals' equipment rentals gross margin increased 1.5 percentage points to 42.6 percent as compared to the same period in 2014, primarily reflecting cost improvements, partially offset by a 0.5 percentage point decrease in time utilization.

We have trained over 3,000 employees, approximately 75 percent of our district managers and over 60 percent of our branch managers on the Lean kaizen process. We continue to implement this program across our branch network, with the objectives of: reducing the cycle time associated with renting our equipment to customers; improving invoice accuracy and service quality; reducing the elapsed time for equipment pickup and delivery; and improving the effectiveness and efficiency of our repair and maintenance operations;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving through both cost reduction and efficiency initiatives, as well as Lean management techniques, with evidence of increased gross margins and enhanced service quality.
